template_name: UK AI, Copyright and the Creative Industries Guidance
source_legislation: House of Lords Communications and Digital Committee: AI, Copyright and the Creative Industries (March 2026)

provisions.normative_text_ref: UK AI, Copyright and the Creative Industries — Recommendation: Mandatory Training Data Disclosure
provisions.description: AI model providers should disclose information about their training data including data sources, content categories, and scope of data used, through both public and regulator-confidential disclosure channels

sub_provisions.description: Training data disclosures should cover the scope of data used, content types, and summary statistics
More detailed disclosures may be required for regulatory examination, beyond public disclosure

evaluation.message: At least one data_provenance record is required — the UK House of Lords report recommends mandatory disclosure of training data sources and characteristics
At least one transparency_disclosure record is required — the UK report recommends public transparency about AI training data and system capabilities
Data provenance records should specify source_type (licensed, scraped, public_domain, synthetic) to support the UK training data disclosure requirements
Data provenance records should be linked to a subject entity to identify which AI model the training data disclosure pertains to

provisions.normative_text_ref: UK AI, Copyright and the Creative Industries — Recommendation: Rights Reservation and Opt-Out
provisions.description: AI model providers should comply with rights reservation mechanisms including TDM opt-out signals, robots.txt, and other rights reservation protocols established by content creators and rights-holders

evaluation.message: At least one copyright_rights_reservation record is required — the UK report recommends compliance with rights reservation mechanisms including TDM opt-out signals
Copyright rights reservation records should specify the opt_out_detection_method used (robots.txt, TDMRep, HTTP headers) for rights reservation compliance verification
Copyright rights reservation records should be linked to a subject entity to identify which AI model's training data the rights compliance applies to

provisions.normative_text_ref: UK AI, Copyright and the Creative Industries — Recommendation: Provenance Standards Compliance
provisions.description: AI model providers should implement and comply with established provenance standards for AI-generated content, enabling transparent identification and verification of content origin

evaluation.message: At least one transparency_marking record is required — the UK report recommends compliance with provenance standards for AI-generated content identification
Transparency marking records should specify marking_scheme_id to identify the provenance standard applied (e.g., C2PA, IPTC) per UK provenance standards recommendations

provisions.normative_text_ref: UK AI, Copyright and the Creative Industries — Recommendation: Licensing with Rights-Holders
provisions.description: AI model providers should establish and document licensing agreements with rights-holders for copyrighted content used in training, including terms, scope, and compliance verification

evaluation.message: At least one license_record is required — the UK report recommends documented licensing agreements with rights-holders for copyrighted training content
License records should specify the licensor to establish which rights-holder granted the training data license
License records should specify the scope of the licensing agreement to verify coverage of intended training uses per UK recommendations
